mc.label: Q3
mc.question: 1. What is the largest country in the world in terms of area?

mc.label: Q4
mc.question: 2. Which end of the Panama Canal is farther east?

mc.label: Q5
mc.question: 3. Which country has the largest number of contiguous neighbors?

mc.label: Q7
mc.question: 5. Which of the following cities is the farthest north?

mc.label: Q8
mc.question: 6. Which of the following cities is in the Southern Hemisphere (check as many as apply)?

mc.label: Q9
mc.question: 7. Which of the following cities lies farthest to the east?

items.fib: 8. How many countries do NOT belong to the United Nations?
9. What's the largest entirely landlocked country?
10. What's the country that covers the greatest latitude range?

documents.document: You're going to have to work a little harder! Study the correct answers and try again.

GEOGRAPHY EXAM RESULTS
Examinee: Date:
Dear ,
Your score on the Geography test was %.
Congratulations on a perfect score. No need to show you the answers!

Here are the correct test answers.
1) What is the largest country in the world in terms of area?
Russia. This isn't even close. Look at a map. And this is AFTER they lost much of their territory in the breakup of the Soviet Union.
2) Which end of the Panama Canal is farther east?
Surprisingly, it's the Pacific end. Panama runs mostly east and west. The Canal runs from the Gulf end in the Northwest to the Pacific end in the Southeast.
3) Which country has the largest number of contiguous neighbors?
That would be China. The breakup of the old Soviet Union added several new ones.
4) How many?
There are 14! Going clockwise they are: Vietnam, Laos, Burma, India, Bhutan, Nepal, Pakistan, Afghanistan, Tajikistan, Kyrgystan, Kazakhstan, Mongolia, Russia, and North Korea.
5) Which of the following cities is the farthest north?
It's Rome, Italy. Blame it on the Gulf Stream.
6) Which of the following cities is in the Southern Hemisphere?
Nairobi and Djakarta. The others are just warm.
7) Which of the following cities lies farthest to the East?
It's Santiago, Chile. South America lies almost entirely east (and south) of North America.
8) How many countries do NOT belong to the United Nations?
You could answer 1 or 2, depending on whether you accept Taiwan as a separate country or not. The other non-member is the Vatican.
9) What's the largest entirely landlocked country?
Kazakhstan, by a country mile.
10) What's the country that covers the greatest latitude range?
It's the USA, thanks to Hawaii and Alaska. If you only counted the 48 contiguous states, the winner would be Canada.
